findings.description: run_with_retries resets `attempt = 0` every iteration right before `attempt += 1`, so the top-of-loop check `attempt < MAX_RETRIES` is permanently `1 < 3`; a persistently failing job loops forever and `return False` (line 72) is unreachable.
Because `attempt` is reset to 0 each iteration (line 69), the intended backoff `RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (attempt + 1)` is frozen at *1 (always 2.0s) — the linear/escalating backoff never grows.

poll_loop is declared `async` but calls blocking `time.sleep()` (and blocking `run_with_retries`) and never `await`s anything, so it monopolizes the event loop and starves all other coroutines instead of yielding via `await asyncio.sleep()`.

`stop_after` is compared against the cumulative instance counters `self.processed + self.failed`, which are never reset; if the worker already handled jobs (e.g. a prior drain_sync), poll_loop can satisfy the stop condition after processing far fewer than `stop_after` new jobs (even one).

drain_sync returns the cumulative instance counters rather than counts for this drain; a second drain_sync call reports totals that include all prior calls, which is almost certainly not the per-invocation result the CLI caller expects.

Any exception raised inside execute_job (e.g. non-serializable payload at line 46 json.dumps, or non-numeric/non-iterable `values` at line 56 sum) is caught broadly and fed into the retry path, which combined with the line-69 bug means a malformed/poison job retries forever instead of failing fast.

aggregate does `sum(values)` on untrusted payload with no type/shape validation: `values` as a string yields wrong/garbage results or `values` as a scalar (e.g. int) raises TypeError, propagating into the retry loop.
convert builds `f"convert {src} {dest}"` from payload and runs it with subprocess shell=True — classic shell-injection sink (note in passing; defer detail to critic-security).
JobQueue.pop uses list.pop(0) which is O(n) per dequeue; fine for the in-memory stub but a scaling foot-gun (note in passing; defer to critic-performance).
